mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns as borrowing costs fall and liquidity expands.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 24-month rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not statistically higher than baseline (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the unemployment rate above 4.5 percent signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in over 65 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5 percent, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE growth exceeds 0.4 percentage points monthly.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in consumer price inflation and contraction in real sales forecasts indicates stagflationary pressure, forcing corporate margin compression and triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth exceeds 3.0 percent while RSAFS remains positive, or Rank IC drops below 0.01 over 36-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and rising corporate revenues, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns within the first week of data release.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 55 percent of observations when INDPRO month-over-month growth exceeds 0.5 percent, or the signal shows no statistical significance during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated policy rates combined with rising unemployment create a restrictive financial environment that suppresses equity risk premiums, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a positive coefficient in out-of-sample regressions, or 60-day returns exceed baseline by more than 2 standard deviations when both conditions are met.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1.2 million units signals weakening residential investment and reduced construction-related demand, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in over 60 percent of triggered instances, or the threshold effect vanishes when HOUST declines are accompanied by rising mortgage applications.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and industrial production confirms broad-based economic expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns within the first week as earnings expectations rise.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 5-day returns in more than 50 percent of observations, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index levels exceeding 240 indicate entrenched inflation that forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in over 65 percent of instances when CPIAUCSL surpasses 240, or the signal loses predictive power when wage growth remains below 3 percent annually.
